question.title: Lombok @Value with public no-args constructor
question.body:
I have the below Occurrence class, i added the @Value annotation to make it immutable, and i set extraPrivate to true for to add a private no-args constructor.

@Builder
@Value
@JsonInclude(JsonInclude.Include.NON_EMPTY)
public class Occurrence {
  .....
}

lombok.config

config.stopBubbling = true
lombok.getter.noIsPrefix = true
lombok.noArgsConstructor.extraPrivate = true

Is there a way to add a public no-args constructor ?

answers.body:
You should annotate the class @NoArgsConstructor(force = true). Using just @NoArgsConstructor will cause a compiler error, because @Value makes the fields final.

From the documentation.

@NoArgsConstructor will generate a constructor with no parameters. If this is not possible (because of final fields), a compiler error will result instead, unless @NoArgsConstructor(force = true) is used, then all final fields are initialized with 0 / false / null.

Also note that:

For fields with constraints, such as @NonNull fields, no check is generated, so be aware that these constraints will generally not be fulfilled until those fields are properly initialized later.

As per my understanding, Lombok @NoArgsConstructor will generate a no arguments/default constructor, by default generated constructor will be public.

@NoArgsConstructor
public class NoArgsDemo {

  private Long id; 
  private String username;
}

From the docs,

@NoArgsConstructor will generate a constructor with no parameters. If this is not possible (because of final fields), a compiler error will result instead, unless @NoArgsConstructor(force = true) is used, then all final fields are initialized with 0 / false / null.

Reference link: Lombok NoArgsConstructor
